title: New York Times Reveals Secret Pentagon UFO Program (AATIP)
detail: The New York Times, in coordination with Politico and The Washington Post, publishes a groundbreaking investigation revealing the existence of the Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program (AATIP), a $22 million Pentagon program that investigated military encounters with UAPs from 2007 to 2012. The story identifies Luis Elizondo as the former program director who resigned from the Pentagon in October 2017 in protest over excessive secrecy. Three declassified Navy videos (FLIR1, Gimbal, GoFast) are released simultaneously through Tom DeLonge's To The Stars Academy of Arts & Science. The story fundamentally reshapes mainstream media coverage of UAPs.

media.caption: Glowing Auras and Black Money: The Pentagon's Mysterious U.F.O. Program
